example.R

smc4578

2026-03-27

## Example feature development in Tidymodels

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.1.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(tidymodels)
## ── Attaching packages ────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels 1.4.1 ──
## ✔ broom        1.0.9     ✔ rsample      1.3.2
## ✔ dials        1.4.2     ✔ tailor       0.1.0
## ✔ infer        1.1.0     ✔ tune         2.0.1
## ✔ modeldata    1.5.1     ✔ workflows    1.3.0
## ✔ parsnip      1.4.1     ✔ workflowsets 1.1.1
## ✔ recipes      1.3.1     ✔ yardstick    1.3.2
## ── Conflicts ───────────────────────────────────────── tidymodels_conflicts() ──
## ✖ scales::discard() masks purrr::discard()
## ✖ dplyr::filter()   masks stats::filter()
## ✖ recipes::fixed()  masks stringr::fixed()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()      masks stats::lag()
## ✖ yardstick::spec() masks readr::spec()
## ✖ recipes::step()   masks stats::step()
library(ranger)

dat0 <- read_csv("pmFRM.csv.gz")
## Rows: 1217 Columns: 2
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## dbl  (1): pmFRM
## date (1): date
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
dat0
## # A tibble: 1,217 × 2
##    date       pmFRM
##    <date>     <dbl>
##  1 2017-01-25  5.91
##  2 2017-02-08  7.93
##  3 2017-02-12  3.91
##  4 2017-02-13  3.93
##  5 2017-02-15  5.75
##  6 2017-02-19  3.75
##  7 2017-03-04  6.22
##  8 2017-03-15 11.3 
##  9 2017-03-21  8.61
## 10 2017-03-23  6.91
## # ℹ 1,207 more rows
## Take a look at the data in a scatterplot
dat0 |>
    ggplot(aes(date, pmFRM)) +
    geom_point()

## Add features based on date
dat0 <- dat0 |> 
  mutate(month = month(date),
         year = year(date)) 

## Split data into training and testing
dat_split <- initial_split(dat0)
dat_split
## <Training/Testing/Total>
## <912/305/1217>
## Get the training data
dat <- training(dat_split)

## Basic linear model
rec <- dat |>
    recipe(pmFRM ~ date)

model <- linear_reg() |>
    set_engine("lm") |>
    set_mode("regression")

wf <- workflow() |>
    add_recipe(rec) |>
    add_model(model)

folds <- dat |>
    vfold_cv(v = 5)

result <- wf |>
    fit_resamples(resamples = folds)

result
## # Resampling results
## # 5-fold cross-validation 
## # A tibble: 5 × 4
##   splits            id    .metrics         .notes          
##   <list>            <chr> <list>           <list>          
## 1 <split [729/183]> Fold1 <tibble [2 × 4]> <tibble [0 × 4]>
## 2 <split [729/183]> Fold2 <tibble [2 × 4]> <tibble [0 × 4]>
## 3 <split [730/182]> Fold3 <tibble [2 × 4]> <tibble [0 × 4]>
## 4 <split [730/182]> Fold4 <tibble [2 × 4]> <tibble [0 × 4]>
## 5 <split [730/182]> Fold5 <tibble [2 × 4]> <tibble [0 × 4]>
## Look at performance metrics
result |>
    collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 2 × 6
##   .metric .estimator    mean     n std_err .config        
##   <chr>   <chr>        <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 rmse    standard   3.93        5 0.0956  pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 rsq     standard   0.00836     5 0.00394 pre0_mod0_post0
################################################################################
## Add new features based on date

rec <- dat |> 
    select(pmFRM, month, year) |> 
    recipe(pmFRM ~ .) 

rec
## 
## ── Recipe ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## 
## ── Inputs 
## Number of variables by role
## outcome:   1
## predictor: 2
## Look at what new features look like
rec |>
    prep(dat) |>
    bake(new_data = NULL)
## # A tibble: 912 × 3
##    month  year pmFRM
##    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1     6  2018  5.22
##  2     9  2019  9.02
##  3     2  2019  7.70
##  4     1  2018 10.6 
##  5    11  2021 10.5 
##  6     8  2019  3.75
##  7     1  2019  5.75
##  8     9  2021  7.63
##  9    11  2017  9.32
## 10    11  2019 10.8 
## # ℹ 902 more rows
## Update workflow
wf <- workflow() |>
    add_recipe(rec) |>
    add_model(model)

folds <- dat |>
    vfold_cv(v = 5)
result <- wf |>
    fit_resamples(resamples = folds)

## Look at performance metrics
result |>
    collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 2 × 6
##   .metric .estimator   mean     n std_err .config        
##   <chr>   <chr>       <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 rmse    standard   3.91       5  0.150  pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 rsq     standard   0.0230     5  0.0117 pre0_mod0_post0
################################################################################
## Change methods

## Use random forest instead
model <- rand_forest() |>
    set_mode("regression") |>
    set_engine("ranger")

## Update workflow
wf <- workflow() |>
    add_recipe(rec) |>
    add_model(model)

wf
## ══ Workflow ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## Preprocessor: Recipe
## Model: rand_forest()
## 
## ── Preprocessor ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## 0 Recipe Steps
## 
## ── Model ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Random Forest Model Specification (regression)
## 
## Computational engine: ranger
folds <- dat |>
    vfold_cv(v = 5)
result <- wf |>
    fit_resamples(resamples = folds)

## Look at performance metrics
result |>
    collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 2 × 6
##   .metric .estimator  mean     n std_err .config        
##   <chr>   <chr>      <dbl> <int>   <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 rmse    standard   3.67      5  0.235  pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 rsq     standard   0.143     5  0.0267 pre0_mod0_post0
################################################################################

## Fit random forest model to the entire training data
model_fit <- wf |>
    fit(data = dat)

## Preparing trainig
dat_prep <- rec |>
    prep(dat) |>
    bake(new_data = NULL)

## Get predicted values on the training data
model_fit |>
    extract_fit_parsnip() |>
    augment(dat_prep)
## # A tibble: 912 × 4
##    .pred month  year pmFRM
##    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1  8.87     6  2018  5.22
##  2  8.47     9  2019  9.02
##  3  6.63     2  2019  7.70
##  4  7.65     1  2018 10.6 
##  5 10.0     11  2021 10.5 
##  6  8.15     8  2019  3.75
##  7  7.19     1  2019  5.75
##  8  9.28     9  2021  7.63
##  9  8.51    11  2017  9.32
## 10  8.53    11  2019 10.8 
## # ℹ 902 more rows
## Plot predicted values vs. true values
model_fit |>
    extract_fit_parsnip() |>
    augment(dat_prep) |>
    ggplot(aes(.pred, pmFRM)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1)

testing <- testing(dat_split) |> 
  mutate(month = month(date),
         year = year(date))

test_prep <- rec |>
    prep(dat) |>
    bake(new_data = testing)
test_prep
## # A tibble: 305 × 3
##    month  year pmFRM
##    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1     3  2017  6.22
##  2     3  2017 11.3 
##  3     3  2017  8.61
##  4     3  2017  9.72
##  5     4  2017  4.52
##  6     6  2017 13.7 
##  7     6  2017 15.1 
##  8     6  2017 11.1 
##  9     7  2017  5.75
## 10     8  2017  8.25
## # ℹ 295 more rows
## Predicted values in the testing data
model_fit |>
    extract_fit_parsnip() |>
    augment(test_prep)
## # A tibble: 305 × 4
##    .pred month  year pmFRM
##    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
##  1  6.99     3  2017  6.22
##  2  6.99     3  2017 11.3 
##  3  6.99     3  2017  8.61
##  4  6.99     3  2017  9.72
##  5 10.3      4  2017  4.52
##  6  9.89     6  2017 13.7 
##  7  9.89     6  2017 15.1 
##  8  9.89     6  2017 11.1 
##  9  8.96     7  2017  5.75
## 10  8.17     8  2017  8.25
## # ℹ 295 more rows
## Plot predicted values vs. true values in the testing data
model_fit |>
    extract_fit_parsnip() |>
    augment(test_prep) |>
    ggplot(aes(.pred, pmFRM)) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1)

## Final fit
wf |>
    last_fit(dat_split) |>
    collect_metrics()
## # A tibble: 2 × 4
##   .metric .estimator .estimate .config        
##   <chr>   <chr>          <dbl> <chr>          
## 1 rmse    standard       4.27  pre0_mod0_post0
## 2 rsq     standard       0.177 pre0_mod0_post0
